latest.title: Total Voting Rights
latest.content:
Today 07:00
RNS Number : 7015Y
World Chess PLC
31 March 2026
World Chess Plc
("World Chess" or the "Company")
Total Voting Rights
In accordance with the FCA's Disclosure Guidance and Transparency Rules, as at 31 March 2026, the Company's issued share capital consists of
1,089,806,579
Ordinary Shares of £0.0001, each with one voting right. The Company does not hold any Ordinary Shares in treasury. Therefore, the total number of voting rights in the Company is
1,089,806,579
.
The above figure of
1,089,806,579
should be used by shareholders in the Company as the denominator for the calculations by which they will determine if they are required to notify their interest in, or a change to their interest in, the share capital of the Company under the Financial Conduct Authority's Disclosure Guidance and Transparency Rules.
For more information, visit:
www.worldchess.com
or contact:
World Chess
Ilya Merenzon, CEO
merenzon@worldchess.com
AlbR Capital Limited - Financial Adviser
David Coffman / Dan Harris
+44 (0) 20 7469 0930
Notes to Editors
World Chess (LSE: CHSS) is a leading chess gaming and entertainment company and Fédération Internationale des Échecs ('FIDE') official commercial partner. World Chess reinvents the sport for the modern consumer by developing worldchess.com, the exclusive platform to play for the FIDE-recognized rating online, running Armageddon, the chess league for prime-time television, and a new kind of chess clubs. The company organized the World Chess Championship Matches in New York and London and signed some of the biggest chess media deals in history. The company's shares are traded on London Stock Exchange: LSE:CHSS. More at worldchess.com.

latest.title: Share Admission Application & Total Voting Rights
latest.content:
Today 12:35
RNS Number : 9380X
World Chess PLC
24 March 2026
World Chess Plc
("World Chess" or the "Company")
Share Admission Application & Total Voting Rights
World Chess Plc (LSE:CHSS) is pleased to announce that 201,868,099 new ordinary shares of £0.0001 each (the "Subscription Shares") have now been issued in respect of the previously announced subscription agreements.
Application is being made for the 201,868,099 Subscription Shares to be admitted to trading on the Main Market of the London Stock Exchange which is expected to be on or around 30 March 2026. These shares rank pari passu with the existing ordinary shares of the Company. Following the issue of the 201,868,099 Shares, the Company's issued ordinary share capital shall consist of 1,089,806,579 ordinary shares.
This figure of 1,089,806,579 represents the total voting rights in the Company and should be used by shareholders as the denominator for the calculation by which they can determine if they are required to notify their interest in, or a change to their interest in, the Company under the Financial Conduct Authority's Disclosure Guidance & Transparency Rules.
World Chess Plc

latest.title: Subscription Agreement
latest.content:
Today 09:10
RNS Number : 5361U
World Chess PLC
26 February 2026
26 February 2026
World Chess Plc
("World Chess" or the "Company")
Subscription Agreement
World Chess Plc (LSE: CHSS), the London-listed chess company and official commercial partner of FIDE, is pleased to announce that it has entered a binding subscription arrangement with two of the Company's existing shareholders (together, the "Investors"), raising approximately £1,154,941 (the "Subscription").
Under the terms of the respective agreements, the Company will issue a total of 175,915,198 new ordinary shares of £0.0001 each (the "Subscription Shares") at an issue price of £0.00656533 per share (the "Issue Price"). The Subscription Shares will be allotted and issued fully paid and will rank pari passu with the Company's existing ordinary shares in all respects.
Completion of the Subscription is subject to the shareholder authority to issue shares to be proposed at a general meeting of the Company being held on 18 March 2026. Should such authority not be obtained, the number of shares to be issued to the Investors will be reduced to 127,605,998, being the current remaining share issuance authority.
As part of this transaction, the period for the fulfilment of the conditions of the warrants referred to in the Company's announcement dated 23 July 2025 will be extended by two years, from 31 December 2026 to 31 December 2028.
Ilya Merenzon, Chief Executive Officer of World Chess Plc, commented:
"This new capital allows us to fund our next phase of growth, and the continued commitment of our existing shareholders gives us both the resources and the confidence to deliver on it."
This announcement contains information which, prior to its disclosure, was inside information as stipulated under Regulation 11 of the Market Abuse (Amendment) (EU Exit) Regulations 2019/310 (as amended).
For more information, visit: www.worldchess.com or contact:
World Chess Plc
Ilya Merenzon, CEO
merenzon@worldchess.com
AlbR Capital Limited
Financial Adviser: David Coffman / Dan Harris
+44 (0) 20 7469 0930

company_30s.what_they_do: World Chess Plc runs the global chess ecosystem. They license the World Chess brand, run official tournaments, sell streaming rights, and partner with schools and tech firms to grow chess as a mainstream sport.
company_30s.why_they_matter: Chess is a growing e‑sports market, especially in Asia and Europe. The company’s official partnership with FIDE gives it exclusive rights to broadcast major events, making it the go‑to platform for fans, sponsors and advertisers looking to tap into a dedicated, family‑friendly audience.
company_30s.current_state: The firm is still very early in its monetisation journey. It has not yet posted significant revenue and is burning cash to fund tournaments and tech development. The share price is a tiny fraction of its all‑time high, trading in pennies and reflecting a market that has lost faith in its ability to turn the chess boom into profit.

rns_narrative: The company has been busy with governance and capital‑raising moves – a new board has been appointed, a share admission application is in progress, and a subscription agreement was signed to bring in fresh cash.
These events signal a shift from a pure fan‑centric model to a revenue‑focused one: the subscription deal will bring in equity and the board changes suggest a new strategy to stabilise the business.

If the subscription closes and the new board delivers on its promises, the company could finally move beyond the funding stage and start generating cash flow from its tournaments and streaming licences.

crowd_tape.dominant_theme: Bleak but hopeful – most chatter is negative, yet a few voices hint at a potential rebound if the new capital takes effect.
crowd_tape.bull_narrative: No clear bullish narrative exists yet. The only hint comes from a post that the upcoming subscription could inject the needed cash to keep the company afloat.
crowd_tape.bear_narrative: Bears point to board resignations, the risk of dilution from the new shares, and a lack of any clear revenue model. They fear the company will keep burning money without a clear path to profitability.
crowd_tape.sentiment_shift: Sentiment has stayed flat and very negative, with no new voices emerging. The lack of discussion is a red flag for a contrarian, but also a sign that the market has moved on and any good news could surprise.

whats_happening_now.attention_check: Google Trends for the ticker is dead – no recent spikes in search volume, which means the market is not currently focused on the company.

near_term_catalysts: The subscription agreement is expected to close in the next 4‑6 weeks. A successful close would raise equity and give the board a mandate to execute a new growth plan.
The board’s new appointments could lead to a fresh strategy that prioritises monetising the streaming platform and securing sponsorships for upcoming tournaments.
If the share admission is approved, the company could see a short‑term volume spike that might lift the price above its 20‑day low.
expect_next.watch_for: Look for a volume surge that exceeds the average daily volume by at least 30 % and a price break above the 20‑day moving average. Also monitor any press releases about sponsorship deals or new tournament licences.
expect_next.timing_regime: The stock appears to be in the early recovery phase after a deep capitulation. The APEX score suggests a tight compression, which is a typical pre‑bounce signal. Confirmation will come when the share price starts to move above its recent lows with solid volume.

buy_vs_sell.bull_case: If the company completes the subscription and the board delivers on its strategy, the price could climb back to roughly half of its peak value. That would represent a 100‑plus % upside from today’s level, while the downside is capped by the current price because the stock is already at a 90 % drop from its high.
buy_vs_sell.bear_case: The main risk is that the subscription fails or that the new capital is diluted by further share issuances. The company also has no proven revenue streams, so it could keep burning cash without a clear exit. Worst‑case, the price could stay stuck near zero if the market loses any remaining confidence.
buy_vs_sell.risk_reward: Potential upside: +200 % or more if the price rebounds to 50 % of its all‑time high. Potential downside: -90 % (the stock is already that far down). The asymmetry is highly favourable for a contrarian play.

playbook.if_bullish: Enter when the price breaks above its 20‑day low with at least a 30 % volume increase. Size the position so that the stop is 15‑20 % below that low – this keeps risk limited while allowing upside. Adjust the stop to the 50‑day low if the price stays above it for a week.
playbook.if_watching: Hold a small position until the subscription deal is officially announced or until a clear sponsorship deal is signed. Watch for a volume spike that indicates institutional interest.
playbook.if_passing: If the company is delisted, files for bankruptcy, or the subscription fails to close, consider liquidating the position to avoid further loss.

ai_insights.bottom_line: A deep crash with no recent sentiment, coupled with an upcoming subscription and board overhaul, makes CHSS a high‑reward, low‑downside contrarian play – act now if the capital raise closes.
